svg.title: Photosynthesis
svg.desc: A 5-scene visual lesson on photosynthesis — light capture, water transport, carbon fixation, glucose production, and the oxygen output.

g.text: Plants absorb sunlight and turn it into chemical energy stored as glucose.
Carbon dioxide + Water + Sunlight → Sugar + Oxygen
Chlorophyll molecules inside chloroplasts absorb red and blue light.
Water molecules are split → releasing O₂ as a by-product
Light energy is stored as ATP + NADPH for the next stage
This is why plants reflect GREEN light — they don't absorb it
Carbon dioxide enters through tiny pores. Water is absorbed through the roots.
ATP energy is used to fix CO₂ into glucose inside the stroma.
The Calvin Cycle runs in the stroma (fluid inside chloroplast)
3 CO₂ + 9 ATP + 6 NADPH → 1 G3P (building block of glucose)
Two G3P molecules combine to form one glucose — the plant's food.
Photosynthesis feeds every living thing and produces the oxygen we breathe.
Without photosynthesis there would be no oxygen, no food, and no life on Earth.
Plants are the ultimate energy converters — turning starlight into life.
How Plants Convert Light into Food
